raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's investments in distributed solar have disproportionately subsidized solar for non-residential and general market residential properties.",
    "Only $218 million (7%) of the $3 billion budget from the California Solar Initiative Program in 2007 went to low-income housing.",
    "Nearly half of California's solar adopters are in the highest quintile of earners.",
    "Approximately 4 percent of California's solar adopters come from the lowest quintile of earners.",
    "Solar adopters in California generally skew towards block groups with high White population, higher education, and higher credit scores.",
    "SB 1385 aims to create a multi-family housing local solar program for underserved communities.",
    "The multi-family housing local solar program proposed in SB 1385 will bring solar and storage systems to multi-unit dwellings.",
    "The program created by SB 1385 will ensure that low-income customers can access affordable and clean energy without cost shifting to other ratepayers."
  ]
}
```
